tabs.dismissFromAiBatch: Keep — remove from AI cleanup suggestion
tabs.keyboardHint: Tip: focus the grid, then ↑↓ to move · Space to select · Enter to switch · Delete to close · ⌘A to select all

suggestions.readyEmpty: Nothing meets the bar right now — keep browsing and the model will surface things again.
suggestions.warmup: Still learning your patterns. Suggestions show up after the model has seen enough of your activity.

cleanup.empty: Nothing to clean up. Inbox-zero for tabs.
cleanup.warmup: Still learning when you abandon tabs. Once the model has seen enough closes, it will start flagging stale ones.
cleanup.smartTooltip: Auto-select tabs the model thinks you won't need soon. Uncheck any false positives — the model learns from your corrections.
cleanup.smartFound_one: Selected {{count}} tab the model wants to close — uncheck any you want to keep.
cleanup.smartFound_other: Selected {{count}} tabs the model wants to close — uncheck any you want to keep.
cleanup.smartNoMatches: Model isn't confident about closing any of your open tabs right now.

workspaces.suggestNameTooltip: Suggest a name with on-device Gemini Nano
workspaces.empty: No workspaces yet. Save the current set of tabs to come back to them later.

insights.totalFocus: {{hours}} h of focus in the last 30 days

toasts.modelsReset: Models reset. They will retrain from your existing event log.
toasts.forestRetrained: Random Forest fit on {{samples}} samples ({{pos}} positive, {{neg}} negative).
toasts.sequenceRebuilt: Rebuilt sequence memory: {{observed}} transitions across {{keys}} domains.
toasts.lrReplayed: Replayed {{open}} open / {{cleanup}} close events through the LR heads.
toasts.aggregatesRebuilt: Aggregates rebuilt — domain stats and co-occurrence are fresh from the events table.

ai.title: Augur AI
ai.subtitle: On-device · Gemini Nano
ai.welcomeTitle: Ask anything — it stays on your device
ai.welcomeBody: Powered by Chrome's built-in Gemini Nano. No API key, no network calls, no logs leave the browser. Conversations clear automatically after 30 minutes of inactivity.
ai.unsupportedTitle: Built-in AI isn't available in this browser
ai.unsupportedBody: Augur AI uses Chrome's built-in Prompt API (Chrome 138+ on desktop). Update Chrome or try this on a supported channel.
ai.unavailableTitle: Gemini Nano can't run on this device
ai.unavailableBody: The on-device model needs ~22 GB of free disk space and a recent GPU. Free up space or check chrome://on-device-internals.
ai.downloadingBody: Gemini Nano is downloading to your device. This happens once — future chats are instant and offline.
ai.statusDownloadable: First message will download the model

pins.heading: PINNED
pins.smartSortHint: Reordered by time-of-day · paused for a few hours after you drag

onboarding.title: Welcome to Augur
onboarding.subtitle: A new tab page that learns from how you actually browse

privacy.title: Stays on your device
privacy.body: All events, model weights, and aggregates live in your browser's IndexedDB. No telemetry, no cloud sync, no remote calls.

collect.title: What gets observed
collect.body: Tab opens, focuses, blurs, closes, navigations, and idle transitions — used only to score what to recommend and what to clean up.

suggestRow.title: Smart suggestions
suggestRow.body: An online model ranks sites you're likely to want next, based on time, day, recency, and what's on screen.

cleanupRow.title: Cleanup that adapts
cleanupRow.body: Keep / close / snooze each suggestion to teach the model. It stops nagging about pages you always keep.

shortcutRow.title: Open Augur in one keystroke
shortcutRow.body: Press ⌘T (macOS) or Ctrl+T (Windows / Linux) to open a new tab — Augur loads instantly.

onboarding.progressHint: Recommendations get sharper after about 50 events.

homepage.title: Make Augur your homepage
homepage.copyFailed: Could not copy — paste it manually from the field.
homepage.homeButtonTitle: Use it as the home button
homepage.homeButtonBody: Open Chrome settings, enable the home button, and paste the URL above as a custom address.
homepage.startupBody: In Chrome's startup settings, choose "Open a specific page" and paste the URL above.
homepage.shortcutBody: Press ⌘T on macOS or Ctrl+T on Windows / Linux to open a new tab — Augur loads instantly. To jump back to it from any page, ⌘1 / Ctrl+1 cycles through your pinned tabs.

settings.predictionsSubtitle: On-device, deterministic, works in every browser.
settings.aiReadyBody: Runs locally via Chrome's built-in Gemini Nano. Prompts and responses never leave your device.
settings.aiDownloadableBody: Chrome's Gemini Nano downloads once on first use (~2–3 GB), then runs locally and offline.
settings.aiUnavailableBody: These features need a Chromium browser (Chrome 138+) that can run Gemini Nano. Not available on Firefox, Safari, or where the model can't be downloaded. Everything else in Augur — predictions, cleanup, search, workspaces — works fully without it.
settings.aiAssistantHint: Adds a chat button to the top bar, powered by on-device Gemini Nano. Conversations stay on your device and clear after 30 minutes idle.
settings.aiPrivacyNote: All AI runs on-device. Nothing is sent to any server. Predictions and rankings are never AI-generated — they stay 100% deterministic whether or not AI is enabled.
settings.userNameHint: Shown in the top-of-page greeting. Leave empty to skip.
settings.oracleHint: Show prediction hint on new tabs
settings.oracleHintHint: When Augur is confident about what you'll open next, a small capsule appears at the top of the new-tab page with up to three suggestions. Use ←/→ to pick, Enter to open, Esc to dismiss.
settings.smartPinSortHint: Re-orders your pinned row based on the time of day you usually open each site. Pauses for 6 hours after a manual drag.
settings.geminiHelpersHint: Suggests names for saved workspaces and other short text. Off by default.
settings.geminiHelpersUnavailable: Unavailable on this browser. Requires Chrome 138 or later on a device that supports Gemini Nano. Predictions continue to work normally without it.
settings.dataSummary: All data stays on this device. Nothing is uploaded.
settings.backupSubtitle: One file with everything — saved items, the full event history, and your trained models — so a new device is instantly as smart as this one.
settings.exportTooltip: Download one JSON with everything: workspaces, pins, stash, events, feedback, and every trained model (weights, embeddings, sequence memory, calibration). This is your backup AND your move-to-a-new-device file. Includes your browsing history — keep it private.
settings.importTooltip: Restore a JSON backup (full replace), or import a debug-bundle .zip (merge history, deduped)
settings.importNotBackup: That's not an Augur backup file. Use a file from “Export all data”.
settings.debugExportTooltip: A ZIP (split files + schema reference) for sharing with a developer when reporting a model issue. Contains your full browsing history.
settings.debugExportFailed: Couldn't build the developer bundle. Try again.
settings.historyBootstrapTooltip: A cold-start tool, NOT a restore: replays the last 30 days of Chrome's own history into Augur so a fresh install has something to learn from. Use “Import data” instead if you have an Augur backup. Runs once automatically on first install.
settings.historyBootstrapDone: Imported {{events}} events across {{domains}} domains. Re-training the model now.
settings.historyBootstrapSkipped: Skipped — history was already imported, or none is available.
settings.historyBootstrapFailed: Couldn't read browser history. Verify that Augur still has the History permission.
settings.privacyHint: Erasing removes every event, model weight, saved workspace, pinned shortcut, and preference. This cannot be undone.
settings.importMerged: Merged {{count}} history events — warming up models…

debug.embeddingEmpty: No embeddings learned yet. Browse for a while, or retrain manually.
debug.manualTrainingHint: All models train automatically as you browse. Use these buttons to force a refresh — useful when diagnosing prediction quality or after a model upgrade.
debug.retrainForestTooltip: Rebuild the random forest using the current event log. Normally runs in the background every 8 hours.
debug.rebuildSequenceTooltip: Replay every focus event through the sequence-memory tables. Use after a model upgrade or if the live signal missed events.
debug.replayLRTooltip: Run recent open / close events through the prediction models' implicit training path. Doesn't reset weights — adds more training steps on top.
debug.rebuildAggregatesTooltip: Recompute domain statistics and co-occurrence pairs from the event log. Use if aggregate counts seem out of sync.
debug.evaluationHint: Replays your recent browsing through the current model and measures how often the domain you actually opened ranked in the top results — compared against a no-ML frecency baseline. Run before and after a model change to see whether it helped.
debug.evaluateTooltip: Replay: scores the last ~60 historical switch-opens with the current model. Optimistic (the model has seen this data); use the delta, not the absolute number.
debug.backtestTooltip: Trains a FRESH model on events older than 7 days, then scores the newer ones it never saw — a genuine generalization number (no leakage). Slower. Aggregates/embeddings stay at current state.
debug.evaluateFailed: Evaluation failed — check the extension console.
debug.evaluatedOn: Replay on {{n}} historical opens in {{ms}} ms. Higher is better; Δ is model minus baseline.
debug.backtestedOn: Backtest on {{n}} held-out opens in {{ms}} ms (fresh model, trained only on older events).
debug.mlpHint: A tiny neural network trains in the background alongside the linear model. Off by default — run a backtest first to confirm it beats the linear+forest ensemble before enabling it for live predictions.

commands.rebuildAggregatesHint: Recompute domain stats and co-occurrence from the raw event log
commands.toggleLanguageHint: Switch between English and 中文

declutter.tooltip: Review every stale tab and sweep them in one pass
declutter.title: Declutter tabs
declutter.subtitle: {{stale}} of {{total}} open tabs are stale
declutter.empty: No stale tabs — your tab set is tidy.
